ksa_text: Knowledge of AI conformity assessment and compliance lifecycle management, including conformity assessment procedures, CE marking requirements, notified body engagement, post-market surveillance obligations, and AI value chain liability triggers.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of information technology (IT) supply chain security and risk management policies, requirements, and procedures.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

onet.top_element_label: Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Included: knowledge maps directly to AI Ethics Assessor scope — RSK-specific signals detected ("conformity.{0,10}assess"). STRM composite is strong (0.5265); note that framework scores routinely underweight risk management and governance vocabulary, so text-based signal carries primary weight here. Seniority is appropriate: the KSA describes practice-level execution, not enterprise strategy.

pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: External validation present: 1 match(es) in RSK job postings or certification scope. Competency appears in RSK role descriptions — sufficient to uphold.
